autonomy_budget.summary: Executed the first mutation-backed shallow-fault pass within the planned budget and restored the baseline cleanly.

delta_summary: ADF now carries mutation-backed proof that a real lab-only httpd outage stops cleanly at the Apache or HTTPD edge boundary and recovers without needing deeper auth or module tracing.
handoff_summary: The first controlled shallow-fault validation for ASMS UI is down is now complete and passed.

During the stop window the accepted delegated observer stopped at the Apache or HTTPD edge boundary: httpd was inactive, listeners on 80 and 443 were absent, and both localhost UI probes failed with connection refused.
Rollback restored httpd, the HTTPS listeners, and both localhost UI entry probes to healthy.
The next move is to use this executed proof to tighten the canonical frontline playbook and decide whether another shallow service boundary is worth testing.
highest_risk_observation: Highest risk: canonical_state_integrity still has not run locally, so keep treating this as bounded pack-local evidence rather than broader canonical host-state truth.

memory_tier.summary: Pack-local restart memory for the completed httpd shallow-fault execution step.

memory_validity.summary: Low-confidence active pack restart memory with 12h freshness window.

summary_lines: Do not widen the httpd outage classification into auth/bootstrap or downstream modules when the HTTPS listener is already absent.
Do not combine the first shallow-fault proof with additional service mutations in the same run.

recommended_next_action: Recommended next action: use the completed httpd shallow-fault validation to tighten the frontline ASMS UI is down playbook and then decide whether another shallow service boundary is worth testing.
summary: This restart memory captures the completed httpd shallow-fault validation and its clean rollback.
